canonical_definition: A standard procedure and dataset used to generate corrupted versions of images with perturbations like snow, brightness changes, and motion blur to test model robustness.
papers: Membership Inference Attacks against Large Vision-Language Models
UniBench: Visual Reasoning Requires Rethinking Vision-Language Beyond Scaling
